rulings.Case Title: MARIA ALVARADO et al VS. SERVIO T GOMEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration Or Set Aside Stipulation; Points And Authorities; Declaration; And Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JULY 6, 2007, LINE 2. PLAINTIFF MARIA ALVARADO, ROBERTO MEJIA AND HECTOR ALVARADO'S Motion To Compel Arbitration Or Set Aside Stipulation; and Request For Judicial Notice: MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED. THE PARTIES ARE TO APPEAR AT THE HEARING AND AGREE UPON AN ARBITRATOR; IF THE PARTIES DO NOT AGREE ON THE ARBITRATOR, EACH PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A LIST OF FIVE ARBITRATORS AND THE COURT WILL SELECT THE ARBITRATOR PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 1281.6. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD C POWELL IN THE CAPACITY AS TRUSTEE OF INTERNATIONAL ALLIANCE OF THEATRICAL AND STAGE VS. ALAN BAILEY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JULY 6, 2007, LINE 8. PLAINTIFF EDWARD POWELL, F. CROWLEY, GREG HOLLAND, SCOTT HOUGHTON, JOHN KISER, RICHARD PUTZ AND HOWARD ROFFMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THIS CASE IS NOT PREEMPTED BECAUSE IT ARISES FROM A DISTINCT CONTRACT. CARPENTER'S HEALTH ETC. V MCCRACKEN (2000) 83 CAL. APP. 4TH 1365. THE DEFENSE OF ECONOMIC DISTRESS IS NOT VIABLE BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS NOT ESTABLISHED A WRONGFUL ACT BY PLAINTIFF. RICH V WHILLOCK, INC. (1984) 157 CAL. APP. 3RD. 1154, 1158. DEFENDANT HAD NO OBLIGATION UNDER THE PLAN TO REIMBURSE PLAINTIFF FOR MEDICAL EXPENSES. THERE WAS NO DEBTOR-CREDITOR RELATIONSHIP WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCSF V SWEET (1995) 12 CAL. 4TH 105. DEFENDANT DID HAVE A RIGHT OF SUBROGATION BOTH UNDER THE PLAN AND THE CONTRACT TO RECOVER MEDICAL EXPENSES CAUSED BY A THIRD PARTY. UNDER LEE V STATE FARM 57 CAL. APP. 3RD 458, THE COMMON FUND DOCTRINE CONTROLS. LEE WAS APPROVED IN CCSF V SWEET AND THE RATIONALE IS BASED ON QUINN V STATE OF CALIFORNIA 15 CAL. 3RD 162 (1975). =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: RAFAEL HERNANDEZ VS. STANFORD K. WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (Ccp 438(C)(1)(B)(Ii)+F);
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JULY 6, 2007, LINE 9. DEFENDANT ROBERT EVANS' Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (C.C.P. SECTION 438(C)(1)(B)(Ii)+F) IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN TEN DAYS. PLAINTIFF'S FRAUD CLAIM IS BASED ON AFFIRMATIVE MISREPRESENTATIONS BY WONG AND IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF MAY BE ABLE TO PLEAD FACTS THAT FORM A BASIS FOR EVANS BEING A CONSPIRATOR. HOWEVER, THE PRESENT ALLEGATIONS AS TO EVANS LACK SUFFICIENT DETAIL. =(302/PJM/KD)
